sections.name: Needs
sections.description: Customer reason for the job and confirmed outcome required. Do not include install steps.

sections.name: Working at heights
sections.description: Ladders, scaffold, roof, flue height, or access-at-height requirements only.

sections.name: System characteristics
sections.description: Existing system and proposed system at a high level: what is coming out and what is going in.

sections.name: Components that require assistance
sections.description: Heavy, awkward, asbestos-risk, roof, cylinder, tank, or other items needing extra labour or specialist help.

sections.name: Restrictions to work
sections.description: Access, parking, time limits, permissions, other trades, or anything that may delay the job.

sections.name: External hazards
sections.description: Site safety hazards outside or around the work area.

sections.name: Delivery notes
sections.description: Where to deliver materials, parking/unloading, access route, and customer availability.

sections.name: Office notes
sections.description: Admin, scheduling, utilities, third parties, permissions, and anything agreed with the customer that office must know.

sections.name: New boiler and controls
sections.description: Keep it simple: what is coming out, what is going in, controls, filters, system protection, position, and agreed appliance details.

sections.name: Flue
sections.description: Flue route, terminal, plume kit, roof/wall work, making good, and any customer agreement about flue position.

sections.name: Pipe work
sections.description: Gas, heating, hot/cold, condensate, route, sizes, upgrades, isolation, and reason for changes.

sections.name: Disruption
sections.description: Mess, lifting floors, drilling, boxing, making good, decoration, water off, heating off, and customer impact.

sections.name: Customer actions
sections.description: What the customer has agreed to do before or during the job.

sections.name: Future plans
sections.description: Future work, follow-on visits, deferred items, and anything explicitly not included now.
